title: Moult architecture-boundaries report
description: Typed output contract for `moult boundaries`. One of Moult's two protected APIs. Each finding is a recorded packwerk boundary violation, classified by severity. A violation is a recorded fact (packwerk resolved the constant via Zeitwerk and verified it crosses a declared boundary), so confidence is null and severity carries the signal; nothing here asserts the code is certainly wrong.

schema_version.description: Bumped only on a breaking change to this shape.

configured.description: Whether the project is packwerk-configured (a packwerk.yml exists). When false, findings is empty.

violations.description: Total referencing sites across all groups.

by_type.description: Violation count keyed by violation type.

reason.description: One auditable note behind a finding's severity classification.

occurrence.description: One referencing site of a violation group.

symbol_id.description: Method-level join key ("<path>:<start_line>:<fqname>"), shared with the other contracts. Null in this slice: packwerk's recorded violations are file-keyed (no line numbers), so no enclosing method is resolved. Findings join at path granularity. Kept nullable for forward-compatibility with line-level offenses.

path.description: Root-relative referencing file path; the health files[] join key.

confidence.description: Always null here: a packwerk violation is a recorded fact, not a probabilistic candidate. The per-finding signal is carried by `severity`, never by a manufactured confidence.

severity.description: How architecturally significant the violation kind is. A classification of the recorded fact, never an assertion the code is wrong.

referencing_package.description: The package making the boundary-crossing reference ("." for the root package).

defining_package.description: The package that owns the referenced constant.

constant.description: The constant that crosses the boundary.

reasons.description: The notes behind the severity classification.

occurrences.description: The referencing sites of this violation group; at least one.
